app_store_policy.summary: Interpreted code (e.g. JavaScript) may be downloaded only if:
clauses: (a) Does not change the primary purpose of the application
(b) Does not create a store or storefront for other code or applications
(c) Does not bypass signing, sandbox, or other security features of the OS
allowed: JavaScript OTA updates (runs in sandboxed JS engine)
Feature flags, UI tweaks, bug fixes, content updates
React Native / Cordova / Ionic style JS updates
prohibited: Accessing private APIs or modifying sandbox

novaic_app_config.build_script: (test -d src-tauri/gen/apple || env -u CI tauri ios init) && bash scripts/patch-ios-xcode.sh && env -u CI tauri ios build -- --no-default-features --features mobile

google_play_policy.source: Developer Program Policies - Device and Network Abuse
google_play_policy.summary: Apps may not modify themselves outside Google Play, EXCEPT:
google_play_policy.exception: Code running in a virtual machine or interpreter (e.g. JavaScript in WebView) is explicitly allowed
allowed: JavaScript updates via interpreters (React Native, WebView)
prohibited: Downloading native code (.dex, .jar, .so files) outside Play Store

tauri_updater_mobile_status.official_docs: Lists android and ios as supported platforms
tauri_updater_mobile_status.maintainer_comment: lucasfernog: 'the updater isn't implemented on mobile yet, we'd need to implement the whole flow'
tauri_updater_mobile_status.check_command: check() returns error on mobile - should not be called
tauri_updater_mobile_status.recommendation: Use feature flags to exclude updater at compile time, or runtime check (cfg!(desktop))

build_flow: 1. beforeBuildCommand (npm run build) runs Vite build
3. Tauri bundles dist/ into app during tauri ios build / tauri android build
4. WebView loads from bundled local assets (asset protocol)
tauri_mobile_frontend_packaging.asset_protocol: Local assets served via asset:// (Linux/macOS) or https://asset.localhost (Windows)

alternatives.name: WebView Remote Loading
alternatives.description: Load main WebView from remote HTTPS URL instead of bundled assets
alternatives.implementation: Use WebviewUrl::External() or create Webview with url: 'https://your-cdn.com/app/'
pros: True OTA: update frontend without app store review
Compliant: JavaScript in WebView is allowed by both stores
cons: Need to handle auth/security for remote content
alternatives.compliance: Allowed under Apple 3.3.2 and Google policy (JS in interpreter)

alternatives.name: Hybrid: Local Shell + Remote Content
alternatives.description: App shell loads local HTML/JS that fetches and renders remote content or config
alternatives.implementation: Bundled shell loads minimal bootstrap, then fetches app bundle from CDN and eval/loads it
pros: OTA for business logic and UI
cons: Must ensure no native code in OTA payload

alternatives.name: Version Check + Store Redirect
alternatives.description: Check REST API or JSON for new version; if available, open App Store / Play Store link in external browser
alternatives.implementation: Feature-flag updater plugin on mobile; use custom API + shell.open() or WebView to open store URL
cons: User must manually update via store

alternatives.name: Expo EAS Update / CodePush-style (if Tauri supports)
alternatives.description: Download JS bundle patches and apply to local WebView
alternatives.implementation: Tauri would need to implement: fetch JS bundle from CDN, verify signature, replace/cache, reload WebView
cons: Tauri has no built-in equivalent; would require custom implementation

recommendations.short_term: Do NOT use Tauri updater plugin on mobile; use cfg!(desktop) or #[cfg(desktop)] to exclude. For version prompts, use REST API + open store URL.
recommendations.medium_term: Consider WebView remote loading if OTA is critical: load main window from https://your-domain.com/app/ for instant updates.
recommendations.long_term: Monitor Tauri plugin-updater for mobile implementation; or implement custom JS-bundle OTA similar to CodePush (fetch, verify, cache, reload).
